quote: Whereof one cannot speak, thereof one must be silent.
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein

quote: The limits of my language mean the limits of my world.
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein

quote: If a lion could talk, we could not understand him.
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein

quote: A picture held us captive. And we could not get outside it.
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein

quote: The meaning of a word is its use in the language.
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein

quote: Everyone is the other and no one is himself.
author: Martin Heidegger

quote: We are ourselves the entities to be analyzed.
author: Martin Heidegger

quote: Making itself intelligible is suicide for philosophy.
author: Martin Heidegger

quote: Every man is born as many men and dies as a single one.
author: Martin Heidegger

quote: The most thought-provoking thing is that we are still not thinking.
author: Martin Heidegger

quote: Understanding is never a subjective relation to a given object, but belongs to the effective history of what is understood.
author: Hans-Georg Gadamer

quote: All understanding is interpretation, and all interpretation takes place in the medium of a language.
author: Hans-Georg Gadamer

quote: To understand a text is to come to understand oneself in a kind of dialogue.
author: Hans-Georg Gadamer

quote: It is the tyranny of hidden prejudices that makes us deaf to what speaks to us in tradition.
author: Hans-Georg Gadamer

quote: Man is condemned to be free; because once thrown into the world, he is responsible for everything he does.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

quote: Existence precedes essence.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

quote: One must imagine Sisyphus happy.
author: Albert Camus

quote: In the depth of winter, I finally learned that within me there lay an invincible summer.
author: Albert Camus

quote: Anxiety is the dizziness of freedom.
author: Søren Kierkegaard

quote: Life can only be understood backwards; but it must be lived forwards.
author: Søren Kierkegaard
